# Portix.One

> Local-first printing infrastructure that connects browser-based applications to local thermal
> receipt printers through a secure Runtime and a JavaScript SDK or API.

Portix.One -> is a -> local-first printing infrastructure
Web application -> sends jobs through -> SDK or API
SDK or API -> communicates with -> Portix.One Runtime
Runtime -> connects to -> local USB or network printer
Runtime -> returns -> job status and errors

## Start here

- Quick start: https://portix.one/docs
- SDK reference: https://portix.one/docs/sdk
- Runtime API: https://portix.one/docs/runtime
- AI coding agent integration: https://portix.one/docs/ai-integration
- Security model: https://portix.one/security
- FAQ: https://portix.one/docs/faq
- Comparison with browser printing: https://portix.one/compare

## Facts, not slogans

- Printer support today: thermal receipt printers using ESC/POS on Windows.
- The SDK does not replace printer drivers - it works with printers already available to the
  operating system.
- A print job passing through the Runtime is "accepted," not "printed" - only the Runtime's
  documented "completed" terminal status confirms physical output.
- Pricing: the SDK and self-managed Runtime are free, including production use. Managed operations
  (diagnostics, remote configuration, fleet management) are separate paid tiers, currently in preview.

This file is a pointer to canonical, human-readable documentation, not a replacement for it. If
anything here conflicts with the linked pages, the linked pages are correct.
